parameters.name: PGM
parameters.help:
Program (PGM)

Specifies the bound program that is to be updated.

This is a required parameter.

Qualifier 1: Program

Qualifier 2: Library

parameters.name: MODULE
parameters.help:
Module (MODULE)

Specifies the names of the existing *MODULE objects that are to replace the modules of the same name in the bound program. If two or more modules of the bound program have the same name, the Replacement library (RPLLIB) parameter indicates which is to be replaced.

If the library of the module being replaced is different from the library of the replacing module, the module's library after the update will remain the library the module was in when the program was first created. If the RPLLIB parameter is required to determine which module to replace, the value to be entered in the RPLLIB parameter for this module will not change due to the update. Up to 300 names can be specified.

This is a required parameter.

Single values

Qualifier 1: Module

Qualifier 2: Library

Specify the name of the module that replaces a module of the bound program.

parameters.name: RPLLIB
parameters.help:
Replacement library (RPLLIB)

Specifies the method used to select the module to be replaced when two or more modules of the bound program have the name specified on the MODULE parameter.

parameters.name: BNDSRVPGM
parameters.help:
Bind service program (BNDSRVPGM)

Specifies the service program to examine for exports if import requests to resolve external symbols cannot be met by the modules and service programs of the updated bound program. If the specified service program can resolve external symbols, it is added to the service programs that are bound to the bound program. Up to 300 names can be specified.

You can control the activation of each service program. You can specify whether the referenced service program is activated at the same time as the program program being updated, or is deferred until a procedure exported from the referenced service program is called. Deferring activation may improve your application's performance.

Single values

Element 1: Service program

Qualifier 1: Service program

Qualifier 2: Library

Element 2: Activation

parameters.name: SRVPGMLIB
parameters.help:
Bound *SRVPGM library name (SRVPGMLIB)

Specifies the library name used to resolve to currently bound service programs. A value other than *SAME for this parameter can be specified if the program attribute ALWLIBUPD is *YES.

Note: The service programs that came from the implicit binding directories (system-supplied service programs) are not changed.

parameters.name: BNDDIR
parameters.help:
Binding directory (BNDDIR)

Specifies the binding directory to examine for exports if import requests to resolve external symbols cannot be met by (1) the modules and service programs of the updated bound program or by (2) the service program specified on the BNDSRVPGM parameter. If a module or service program listed in the specified binding directory can resolve external symbols, it is added to the modules or service programs that are bound to the bound program. Up to 300 names can be specified.

Single values

Qualifier 1: Binding directory

Qualifier 2: Library

parameters.name: ACTGRP
parameters.help:
Activation group (ACTGRP)

Specifies the activation group to be used for the updated program.

Note: Changing the activation group name can affect the behavior of the program (or service program). Refer to the ILE Concepts book, SC41-5606 for detailed information on the behavior of named activation groups.

指定できる特殊値: *SAME

parameters.name: OPTION
parameters.help:
Creation options (OPTION)

Specifies the options to be used when the bound program is updated.

You can specify up to 6 values for this parameter.

Creating Program Objects

Duplicate Procedure Names

Duplicate Variable Names

Issuing Diagnostic Messages

Trimming Marooned Module

A marooned module is a module of the bound program being updated. This module was originally bound into the bound program from a binding directory to resolve one or more imports. Imports are not resolved for this program update.

Note: Programs may grow significantly over time when *NOTRIM is specified.

Note: If marooned modules are removed from the bound program for this program update, the exports that the modules contain are not available for other program updates.

Resolving References (Imports)

Note: If this command contains an import that does not resolve, an exception will be generated when the command is run.

parameters.name: DETAIL
parameters.help:
Listing detail (DETAIL)

Specifies the level of detail of the binder listing to be printed. The printer file *LIBL/QSYSPRT is used to create the listing.
